title: A Question of Privilege
content:
آ آ آ آ It was Andrew Jackson Sutter who, despising Mr. Cutter for remarks he heard him utter in debate upon the floor,
آ آ آ آ Swung him up into the skylight, in the peaceful, pensive twilight, and then keerlessly proceeded, makinï؟½ no account what we did To wipe up with his person casual dust upon the floor.

آ آ آ آ Now a square fight never frets me, nor unpleasantness upsets me, but the simple thing that gets me now the job is done and gone,
آ آ آ آ And weï؟½ve come home free and merry from the peaceful cemetery, leavinï؟½ Cutter there with Sutter that mebbee just a stutter On the part of Mr. Cutter caused the loss we deeply mourn.

آ آ آ آ Some bashful hesitation, just like spellinï؟½ punctooation might have worked an aggravation on to Sutterï؟½s mournful mind,
آ آ آ آ For the witnesses all vary ez to wot was said and nary a galoot will toot his horn except the way he is inclined.

آ آ آ آ But they all allow that Sutter had begun a kind of mutter, when uprose Mr. Cutter with a sickening kind of ease,
آ آ آ آ And proceeded then to wade in to the subject then prevadinï؟½: ï؟½Is Profanity degradinï؟½?ï؟½ in words like unto these:

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Onlike the previous speaker, Mr. Sutter of Yreka, he was but a humble seeker and not like him a cussï؟½
آ آ آ آ It was here that Mr. Sutter softly reached for Mr. Cutter, when the latter with a stutter said: ï؟½ac-customed to discuss.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Then Sutter he rose grimly, and sorter smilinï؟½ dimly bowed onto the Chairman primly (just like Cutter ez could be!)
آ آ آ آ Drawled ï؟½he guessed he must fall back as Mr. Cutter owned the pack as he just had played the Jack as ï؟½ (here Cutterï؟½s gun went crack! as Mr. Sutter gasped and ended) ï؟½every man can see!ï؟½

آ آ آ آ But William Henry Pryor just in range of Sutterï؟½s fire here evinced a wild desire to do somebody harm,
آ آ آ آ And in the general scrimmage no one thought if Sutterï؟½s ï؟½imageï؟½ was a misplaced punctooation like the hole in Pryorï؟½s arm.

آ آ آ آ For we all waltzed in together, never carinï؟½ to ask whether it was Sutter or was Cutter we woz tryinï؟½ to abate.
آ آ آ آ But we couldnï؟½t help perceivinï؟½, when we took to inkstand heavinï؟½, that the process was relievinï؟½ to the sharpness of debate,

آ آ آ آ So weï؟½ve come home free and merry from the peaceful cemetery, and I make no commentary on these simple childish games;
آ آ آ آ Things is various and human and the man ainï؟½t born of woman who is free to intermeddle with his palï؟½s intents and aims.
meaning:
Bret Harte (Francis)'s contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "A Question of Privilege"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
